DIA_MOREDG_PORPHYRIE.name: Acute Intermittent Porphyria
DIA_MOREDG_PORPHYRIE.description: Acute intermittent porphyria (AIP) is a rare autosomal dominant metabolic disorder affecting the production of heme resulting from a deficiency of the porphobilinogen deaminase. It is the most common of the acute porphyrias.

DIA_MOREDG_SPHEROCYTOSE.name: Minkowski-Chauffard Disease
DIA_MOREDG_SPHEROCYTOSE.description: Minkowski-Chauffard disease or Hereditary spherocytosis is an abnormality of red blood cells, or erythrocytes. The disorder is caused by mutations in genes relating to membrane proteins that allow for the erythrocytes to change shape. The abnormal erythrocytes are sphere-shaped (spherocytosis) rather than the normal biconcave disk shaped.

DIA_MOREDG_MARCHIAFAVA_MICHELI.name: Marchiafava Micheli Syndrome
DIA_MOREDG_MARCHIAFAVA_MICHELI.description: Marchiafava Micheli syndrome or Paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (PNH) is a rare, acquired, life-threatening disease of the blood characterized by destruction of red blood cells by the complement system, a part of the body's innate immune system.

DIA_MOREDG_FAVISME.name: Favism
DIA_MOREDG_FAVISME.description: Favism or Glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency (G6PDD) is an inborn error of metabolism that predisposes to red blood cell breakdown.

DIA_MOREDG_DEFICIT_PK.name: PK Deficiency
DIA_MOREDG_DEFICIT_PK.description: Pyruvate kinase deficiency is an inherited metabolic disorder of the enzyme pyruvate kinase which affects the survival of red blood cells.

DIA_MOREDG_AHAA.name: Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia
DIA_MOREDG_AHAA.description: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia (AIHA) occurs when antibodies directed against the person's own red blood cells (RBCs) cause them to burst (lyse), leading to an insufficient number of oxygen-carrying red blood cells in the circulation.

DIA_MOREDG_PTT.name: Moschkowitz Disease
DIA_MOREDG_PTT.description: Moschkowitz disease or Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP) is a blood disorder that results in blood clots forming in small blood vessels throughout the body.

DIA_MOREDG_DREPA_A.name: Acute Sickle Cell Disease
DIA_MOREDG_DREPA_A.description: La drépanocytose, aussi appelée anémie falciforme, est une maladie héréditaire touchant l'hémoglobine des globules rouges.

DIA_MOREDG_DREPA_C.name: Chronic Sickle Cell Disease
DIA_MOREDG_DREPA_C.description: La drépanocytose, aussi appelée anémie falciforme, est une maladie héréditaire touchant l'hémoglobine des globules rouges.

DIA_MOREDG_WILLEBRAND.name: Von Willebrand Disease
DIA_MOREDG_WILLEBRAND.description: Von Willebrand disease (vWD) is the most common hereditary blood-clotting disorder in humans. It arises from a deficiency in the quality or quantity of von Willebrand factor (vWF), a multimeric protein that is required for platelet adhesion.

DIA_MOREDG_NIEMANN-PICK.name: Niemann-Pick Disease
DIA_MOREDG_NIEMANN-PICK.description: Niemann-Pick disease type C (NP-C) is a lysosomal lipid storage disease.

DIA_MOREDG_KIMURA.name: Kimura Disease
DIA_MOREDG_KIMURA.description: Kimura disease is a benign and chronic inflammatory disorder of unknown etiology, occurring mainly in Asian countries (very rarely in Western countries) and predominantly affecting young men.

DIA_MOREDG_FANCONI.name: Fanconi Anemia
DIA_MOREDG_FANCONI.description: Fanconi anemia (FA) is a hereditary DNA repair disorder. FA is due to mutations in genes involved in DNA repair and genomic stability. Fifteen genes representing 15 complementation groups have been identified.

DIA_MOREDG_BERNARD-SOULIER.name: Bernard-Soulier Syndrome
DIA_MOREDG_BERNARD-SOULIER.description: Bernard Soulier syndrome (BSS) is an inherited platelet disorder characterized by mild to severe bleeding tendency , macrothrombocytopenia and absent ristocetin-induced platelet agglutination.

DIA_MOREDG_THROMBASTHENIE_GLANDZMANN.name: Glanzmann Thrombasthenia
DIA_MOREDG_THROMBASTHENIE_GLANDZMANN.description: Glanzmann thrombasthenia (GT) is a bleeding syndrome characterized by spontaneous mucocutaneous bleeding and an exaggerated response to trauma due to a constitutional thrombocytopenia.

DIA_MOREDG_ANEMIE_BLACKFAN_DIAMOND.name: Blackfan-Diamond Anemia
DIA_MOREDG_ANEMIE_BLACKFAN_DIAMOND.description: Blackfan-Diamond anemia (DBA) is a congenital aregenerative and often macrocytic anemia with erythroblastopenia. At present, disease-causing mutations are identified in 40-45% of patients. All involved genes code for ribosomal proteins (RPs).

DIA_MOREDG_MALADIE_GAUCHER.name: Gaucher Disease
DIA_MOREDG_MALADIE_GAUCHER.description: Gaucher disease (GD) is a lysosomal storage disorder encompassing three main forms (types 1, 2 and 3), a fetal form and a variant with cardiac involvement (Gaucher disease - ophthalmoplegia - cardiovascular calcification or Gaucher-like disease). GD is due to mutations in the GBA gene (1q21) that codes for a lysosomal enzyme, glucocerebrosidase, or in very rare cases the PSAP gene that codes for its activator protein (saposin C). The deficiency in glucocerebrosidase leads to the accumulation of glucosylceramidase (or beta-glucocerebrosidase) deposits in the cells of the reticuloendothelial system of the liver, the spleen and the bone marrow (Gaucher cells).

DIA_MOREDG_MALADIE_DUNCAN.name: Duncan Disease
DIA_MOREDG_MALADIE_DUNCAN.description: Duncan disease or Purtilo syndrome or X-linked lymphoproliferative disease is a primary immune deficiency linked to cellular immune deficiencies.

DIA_MOREDG_ASIA.name: Acquired Idiopathic Sideroblastic Anemia
DIA_MOREDG_ASIA.description: Acquired idiopathic sideroblastic anemia (AISA) is a rare myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) characterized by ineffective hemopoiesis affecting one or more blood cell lineages (myeloid, erythroid or megakaryocytic) leading to peripheral blood cytopenias and an increased risk of developing leukaemia.

DIA_MOREDG_ERYTHROBLASTOPENIE_ISO.name: Isolated Erythroblastopenia
DIA_MOREDG_ERYTHROBLASTOPENIE_ISO.description: It is an acute blood disorder characterized by the decrease or disappearance of erythroblasts (bone marrow cells specialized in the synthesis of hemoglobin) and resulting in anemia.

DIA_MOREDG_HEMOGLOBINOSE_C.name: Hemoglobin C Disease
DIA_MOREDG_HEMOGLOBINOSE_C.description: Hemoglobin C disease (HbC) is a hemoglobinopathy characterized by production of abnormal variant hemoglobin known as hemoglobin C.

DIA_MOREDG_HEMOGLOBINOSE_D.name: Hemoglobin D Disease
DIA_MOREDG_HEMOGLOBINOSE_D.description: Hemoglobin D disease (HbD) is a hemoglobinopathy characterized by production of abnormal variant hemoglobin known as hemoglobin D.

DIA_MOREDG_HEMOGLOBINOSE_E.name: Hemoglobin E Disease
DIA_MOREDG_HEMOGLOBINOSE_E.description: Hemoglobin E disease (HbE) is a hemoglobinopathy characterized by production of abnormal variant hemoglobin known as hemoglobin E.

DIA_MOREDG_HEMOGLOBINOSE_M.name: Hemoglobin M Disease
DIA_MOREDG_HEMOGLOBINOSE_M.description: Hemoglobin M disease (HbM) is a hemoglobinopathy characterized by production of abnormal variant hemoglobin known as hemoglobin M.

DIA_MOREDG_PLUMMER_VINSON.name: Plummer-Vinson Syndrome
DIA_MOREDG_PLUMMER_VINSON.description: Plummer-Vinson or Paterson-Kelly syndrome presents as a classical triad of dysphagia, iron-deficiency anemia and esophageal webs. The etiopathogenesis of Plummer-Vinson syndrome is unknown. The most important possible etiological factor is iron deficiency. Other possible factors include malnutrition, genetic predisposition or autoimmune processes.

DIA_MOREDG_RENDU_OSLER_WEBER.name: Hereditary Hemorrhagic Telangiectasia
DIA_MOREDG_RENDU_OSLER_WEBER.description: Rendu-Osler-Weber disease or Hereditary Hemorrhagic Telangiectasia (HHT) is an inherited disorder of angiogenesis characterized by mucocutaneous telangiectases and visceral arteriovenous malformations. This genetic disorder is due to pathogenic variants primarily in ENG or ACVRL1, encoding proteins involved in vascular development and angiogenic homeostasis of capillaries.

DIA_MOREDG_SD_EVANS.name: Evans Syndrome
DIA_MOREDG_SD_EVANS.description: A rare chronic hematologic disorder characterized by the simultaneous or sequential association of autoimmune hemolytic anemia with immune thrombocytopenic purpura and occasionally autoimmune neutropenia, in the absence of a known underlying etiology.

DIA_MOREDG_HEMOCHROMATOSE_1.name: Hemochromatosis Type 1
DIA_MOREDG_HEMOCHROMATOSE_1.description: Symptomatic form of hemochromatosis type 1 is a rare, hereditary hemochromatosis characterized by inappropriately regulated intestinal iron absorption which leads to excessive iron storage in various organs and manifests with a wide range of signs and symptoms.

DIA_MOREDG_HEMOCHROMATOSE_2.name: Hemochromatosis Type 2
DIA_MOREDG_HEMOCHROMATOSE_2.description: Hemochromatosis type 2 (juvenile) is the early-onset and most severe form of rare hereditary hemochromatosis (HH), a group of diseases characterized by excessive tissue iron deposition of genetic origin.

DIA_MOREDG_HEMOCHROMATOSE_3.name: Hemochromatosis Type 3
DIA_MOREDG_HEMOCHROMATOSE_3.description: Type 3 hemochromatosis is a form of rare hereditary hemochromatosis (HH), a group of diseases characterized by excessive tissue iron deposition of genetic origin.

DIA_MOREDG_HEMOCHROMATOSE_4.name: Hemochromatosis Type 4
DIA_MOREDG_HEMOCHROMATOSE_4.description: Hemochromatosis type 4 (also called ferroportin disease) is a form of rare hereditary hemochromatosis (HH), a group of diseases characterized by excessive tissue iron deposition of genetic origin.

DIA_MOREDG_APLASIE_MÉDULLAIRE_IDIOPATHIQ.name: Idiopathic Aplastic Anemia
DIA_MOREDG_APLASIE_MÉDULLAIRE_IDIOPATHIQ.description: Idiopathic bone marrow failure or Idiopathic aplastic anemia is an inability of the bone marrow to replace blood cells as they die naturally. It results in a deficit in the production of one or more types of cells normally found in the blood. The name "idiopathic" means that the cause is unknown.

TRM_MOREDG_PURPURA_FULMINANS.name: Purpura Fulminans
TRM_MOREDG_PURPURA_FULMINANS.description: Purpura fulminans is an acute, often fatal, thrombotic disorder which manifests as blood spots, bruising and discolouration of the skin resulting from coagulation in small blood vessels within the skin and rapidly leads to skin necrosis. Purpura fulminans is a presenting feature of severe acute sepsis.

TRM_MOREDG_CIVD.name: Disseminated Intravascular Coagulation
TRM_MOREDG_CIVD.description: Disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC) is a condition in which blood clots form throughout the body, blocking small blood vessels.

TRM_MOREDG_PURPURA_THROMBOPENIQUE.name: Severe Thrombocytopenic Purpura
TRM_MOREDG_PURPURA_THROMBOPENIQUE.description: Severe thrombocytopenia causes purpura: it is petechial, non-infiltrated, isolated or ecchymotic, sometimes associated with large hematomas. The discovery of a purpura requires the prescription of a blood count. The platelet count is usually less than 20 giga/L.

TRM_MOREDG_DRESS_SD.name: DRESS Syndrome
TRM_MOREDG_DRESS_SD.description: Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS) syndrome, initially recognized as a serious form of cutaneous drug adverse reaction, is now viewed as a drug-related syndrome that can cause life-threatening organ dysfunctions.

TRM_MOREDG_THROMBOPENIE_HEPARINE.name: Heparin-Induced Thrombocytopenia
TRM_MOREDG_THROMBOPENIE_HEPARINE.description: Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT) is a rare drug-induced, immune-mediated prothrombotic disorder associated with thrombocytopenia and venous and/or arterial thrombosis. HIT results from a humoral immune reaction directed against a complex involving endogenous platelet factor 4 (PF4) and exogenous heparin: auto-antibodies recognize PF4 only when it is complexed with heparin. This immune complex activates the circulating platelets via their FcγRIIA surface receptors, leading to consumptive thrombocytopenia and hypercoagulability. The source of heparin (bovine >porcine), its formulation (unfractionated >low molecular weight >fondaparinux), the dose (prophylactic >therapeutic >heparin flushes), route of administration (subcutaneous >intravenous) and duration of administration (over 4 days >4 days or less) are determining factors.

DIA_MOREDG_RA.name: Aortic Stenosis
DIA_MOREDG_RA.description: Aortic stenosis is one of the most common and most serious valve disease problems. Aortic stenosis is a narrowing of the aortic valve opening. Aortic stenosis restricts the blood flow from the left ventricle to the aorta and may also affect the pressure in the left atrium.

DIA_MOREDG_IA.name: Aortic Insufficiency
DIA_MOREDG_IA.description: Aortic insufficiency (AI), also known as aortic regurgitation (AR), is the leaking of the aortic valve of the heart that causes blood to flow in the reverse direction during ventricular diastole, from the aorta into the left ventricle. As a consequence, the cardiac muscle is forced to work harder than normal.

DIA_MOREDG_SYNCOPE_NC.name: Neurocardiogenic Syncope
DIA_MOREDG_SYNCOPE_NC.description: Syncope is a brief loss of consciousness (BLC) due to rapid onset cerebral hypoperfusion, of short duration and complete and spontaneous recovery. After the event, the neurological state (including behavior and orientation) is normal. The patient has no recorded memories during the BLC period.

DIA_MOREDG_HYPOTA_ORTHO.name: Orthostatic Hypotension
DIA_MOREDG_HYPOTA_ORTHO.description: Orthostatic hypotension is defined as a decrease in systolic blood pressure of 20 mm Hg or a decrease in diastolic blood pressure of 10 mm Hg within three minutes of standing when compared with blood pressure from the sitting or supine position.

DIA_MOREDG_SD_STOKESADAMS.name: Adams–Stokes Syndrome
DIA_MOREDG_SD_STOKESADAMS.description: Sudden collapse into unconsciousness due to a disorder of heart rhythm in which there is a slow or absent pulse resulting in syncope (fainting) with or without convulsions. In this condition, the normal heartbeat passing from the upper chambers of the heart to the lower chambers is interrupted.

DIA_MOREDG_LIPOTHYMIE.name: Presyncope
DIA_MOREDG_LIPOTHYMIE.description: Lightheadedness can also be called presyncope, in contrast with syncope (fainting), particularly in cases of temporary visual field loss. Lightheadedness is a common and typically unpleasant sensation of dizziness or a feeling that one may faint. The sensation of lightheadedness can be short-lived, prolonged, or, rarely, recurring. In addition to dizziness, the individual may feel as though his or her head is weightless. The individual may also feel as though the room is "spinning" or moving (vertigo) associated with lightheadedness. Most causes of lightheadedness are not serious and either cure themselves quickly, or are easily treated.

DIA_MOREDG_FIBRILLATION_ATRIALE.name: Atrial Fibrillation
DIA_MOREDG_FIBRILLATION_ATRIALE.description: Atrial fibrillation (AF or A-fib) is an abnormal heart rhythm (arrhythmia) characterized by rapid and irregular beating of the atrial chambers of the heart.

DIA_MOREDG_DYSFONCTION_SINUSALE.name: Sick Sinus Syndrome
DIA_MOREDG_DYSFONCTION_SINUSALE.description: Sick sinus syndrome (SSS), is a group of abnormal heart rhythms (arrhythmias) presumably caused by a malfunction of the sinus node, the heart's primary pacemaker. Tachycardia-bradycardia syndrome is a variant of sick sinus syndrome in which the arrhythmia alternates between slow and fast heart rates. Tachycardia-bradycardia syndrome is often associated with ischemic heart disease and heart valve disease.

DIA_MOREDG_BLOC_ATRIOVENTRICULAIRE.name: Atrioventricular Block
DIA_MOREDG_BLOC_ATRIOVENTRICULAIRE.description: Atrioventricular block (AV block) is a type of heart block that occurs when the electrical signal traveling from the atria, or the upper chambers of the heart, to ventricles, or the lower chambers of the heart, is impaired. Normally, the sinoatrial node (SA node) produces an electrical signal to control the heart rate. The signal travels from the SA node to the ventricles through the atrioventricular node (AV node). In an AV block, this electrical signal is either delayed or completely blocked. When the signal is completely blocked, the ventricles produce their own electrical signal to control the heart rate. The heart rate produced by the ventricles is much slower than that produced by the SA node.

DIA_MOREDG_BB_GAUCHE.name: Left Bundle Branch Block
DIA_MOREDG_BB_GAUCHE.description: Left bundle branch block (LBBB) is a cardiac conduction abnormality seen on the electrocardiogram (ECG). In this condition, activation of the left ventricle of the heart is delayed, which causes the left ventricle to contract later than the right ventricle.

DIA_MOREDG_TACHYCARDIE_VENTRICULAIRE.name: Left Bundle Branch Block
DIA_MOREDG_TACHYCARDIE_VENTRICULAIRE.description: Left bundle branch block (LBBB) is a cardiac conduction abnormality seen on the electrocardiogram (ECG). In this condition, activation of the left ventricle of the heart is delayed, which causes the left ventricle to contract later than the right ventricle.

DIA_MOREDG_BOUVERET.name: Bouveret-Hoffmann Syndrome
DIA_MOREDG_BOUVERET.description: It is a rapid heartbeat that originates neither from the atria nor the ventricles, but rather from a node responsible for the propagation of electrical impulses of the heart.

DIA_MOREDG_TORSADE_DE_POINTES.name: Torsade De Pointes
DIA_MOREDG_TORSADE_DE_POINTES.description: Torsades de pointes (TdP) (translated as "twisting of peaks"), is a specific type of abnormal heart rhythm that can lead to sudden cardiac death. It is a polymorphic ventricular tachycardia that exhibits distinct characteristics on the electrocardiogram (ECG). It was described by Frenchman François Dessertenne in 1966.

DIA_MOREDG_SD_WPW.name: Wolff–Parkinson–White Syndrome
DIA_MOREDG_SD_WPW.description: Wolff–Parkinson–White syndrome (WPWS) is a disorder due to a specific type of problem with the electrical system of the heart which has resulted in symptoms.

DIA_MOREDG_MDG_ICG.name: Left-Sides Heath Failure
DIA_MOREDG_MDG_ICG.description: Left-sided heart failure occurs when the left ventricle doesn't pump efficiently.

DIA_MOREDG_MDG_ICD.name: Right-Sided Heath Failure
DIA_MOREDG_MDG_ICD.description: Righ-sided heart failure occurs when the right ventricle doesn't pump efficiently.

DIA_MOREDG_AOMI.name: OALL
DIA_MOREDG_AOMI.description: Obliterating Arteriopathy of the Lower Limbs (OALL) is defined as partial or total atheromatous obstruction of one or more arteries intended for the lower limbs.

DIA_MOREDG_ANEVRISME_AAA.name: Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
DIA_MOREDG_ANEVRISME_AAA.description: Abdominal aortic aneurysm (AAA) is a localized enlargement of the abdominal aorta such that the diameter is greater than 3 cm or more than 50% larger than normal.

DIA_MOREDG_ANEVRISME_POPLITE.name: Popliteal Artery Aneurysms
DIA_MOREDG_ANEVRISME_POPLITE.description: Popliteal artery aneurysms (PAAs) is bulging and weakness in the wall of the popliteal artery, which supplies blood to the knee joint, thigh and calf.

DIA_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MESENTERIQUE_CHRONIQ.name: Chronic Mesenteric Ischemia
DIA_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MESENTERIQUE_CHRONIQ.description: Chronic digestive ischemia affects the elderly; it predominates in women in more than 70% of cases. This pathology is uncommon and indicates an advanced and diffuse atheromatous disease.

TRM_MOREDG_URGENCE_HTA.name: Hypertensive Emergency
TRM_MOREDG_URGENCE_HTA.description: Hypertensive emergency is characterized by hypertension, most often severe (or quickly installed in a normotensive patient) and which is associated with acute damage to the target organs.

TRM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MS.name: Acute Ischemia Of The Upper Limb
TRM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MS.description: Acute limb ischaemia occurs when there is a sudden lack of blood flow to a limb.

TRM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MI.name: Acute Ischemia Of The Lower Limb
TRM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MI.description: Acute limb ischaemia occurs when there is a sudden lack of blood flow to a limb.

TRM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MESENTERIQUE.name: Acute Mesenteric Ischemia
TRM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MESENTERIQUE.description: Mesenteric ischemia is a medical condition in which injury to the small intestine occurs due to not enough blood supply, it's associated with a high risk of death.

TRM_MOREDG_RUPTURE_AAA.name: Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Rupture
TRM_MOREDG_RUPTURE_AAA.description: Rupture of the abdominal aortic aneurysm.

TRM_MOREDG_RUPTURE_AIC.name: Rupture Common Iliac Aneurysm
TRM_MOREDG_RUPTURE_AIC.description: Rupture of the common iliac aneurysm.

TRM_MOREDG_TRANSPLANTATION_COEUR.name: Heart Transplant
TRM_MOREDG_TRANSPLANTATION_COEUR.description: A heart transplant, or a cardiac transplant, is a surgical transplant procedure performed on patients with end-stage heart failure or severe coronary artery disease when other medical or surgical treatments have failed.

DIA_MOREDG_DALA.name: Partial Androgen Deficiency In Aged Male
DIA_MOREDG_DALA.description: The term "andropause" is commonly used to refer to the decline in circulating testosterone from the age of 40 in men. Another popular term is that of "hypogonadism" in the elderly or partial androgen deficiency in the elderly - known by the acronym PADAM (Partial Androgen Deficiency in Aged Male).

DIA_MOREDG_HPRL_F.name: Hyperprolactinemia
DIA_MOREDG_HPRL_F.description: Prolactin is a peptide hormone produced by the anterior pituitary gland that is primarily associated with lactation and plays a vital role in breast development during pregnancy. Hyperprolactinaemia may cause galactorrhea (production and spontaneous flow of breast milk), infertility, and disruptions in the normal menstrual period in women; and hypogonadism, infertility and erectile dysfunction in men.

DIA_MOREDG_HPRL_H.name: Hyperprolactinemia
DIA_MOREDG_HPRL_H.description: Prolactin is a peptide hormone produced by the anterior pituitary gland that is primarily associated with lactation and plays a vital role in breast development during pregnancy. Hyperprolactinaemia may cause galactorrhea (production and spontaneous flow of breast milk), infertility, and disruptions in the normal menstrual period in women; and hypogonadism, infertility and erectile dysfunction in men.

DIA_MOREDG_ACROMEGALIE.name: Acromegaly
DIA_MOREDG_ACROMEGALIE.description: Acromegaly is a disorder that results from excess growth hormone (GH) after the growth plates have closed.

DIA_MOREDG_CUSHING_F.name: Cushing's Disease
DIA_MOREDG_CUSHING_F.description: Cushing's disease is one cause of Cushing's syndrome characterised by increased secretion of adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH) from the anterior pituitary (secondary hypercortisolism). This is most often as a result of a pituitary adenoma (specifically pituitary basophilism) or due to excess production of hypothalamus CRH (corticotropin releasing hormone) (tertiary hypercortisolism/hypercorticism) that stimulates the synthesis of cortisol by the adrenal glands.

DIA_MOREDG_CUSHING_H.name: Cushing's Disease
DIA_MOREDG_CUSHING_H.description: Cushing's disease is one cause of Cushing's syndrome characterised by increased secretion of adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH) from the anterior pituitary (secondary hypercortisolism). This is most often as a result of a pituitary adenoma (specifically pituitary basophilism) or due to excess production of hypothalamus CRH (corticotropin releasing hormone) (tertiary hypercortisolism/hypercorticism) that stimulates the synthesis of cortisol by the adrenal glands.

DIA_MOREDG_ADN_THYREOTRP.name: Central Hyperthyroidism
DIA_MOREDG_ADN_THYREOTRP.description: Central hyperthyroidism is caused by the overproduction of thyroid hormones from pituitary gland. TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone) is a hormone secreted by the anterior part of the pituitary gland. TSH will be produced in excess in the presence of an adenoma sitting in this specific area. TSH will over-stimulate the thyroid gland, located at the base of the neck, resulting in hypersecretion of thyroid hormones.

DIA_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPT_F.name: Anterior Pituitary Insufficiency Syndrome
DIA_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPT_F.description: Syndrome characterized by a deficiency of all the anterior pituitary hormones (panhypopituitarism). The presence of a pituitary adenoma can be at the origin of an anterior pituitary insufficiency.

DIA_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPT_H.name: Anterior Pituitary Insufficiency Syndrome
DIA_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPT_H.description: Syndrome characterized by a deficiency of all the anterior pituitary hormones (panhypopituitarism). The presence of a pituitary adenoma can be at the origin of an anterior pituitary insufficiency.

DIA_MOREDG_ADDISON_F.name: Addison's Disease
DIA_MOREDG_ADDISON_F.description: Addison's disease, also known as primary adrenal insufficiency and hypocortisolism, is a long-term endocrine disorder in which the adrenal glands do not produce enough steroid hormones.

DIA_MOREDG_ADDISON_H.name: Addison's Disease
DIA_MOREDG_ADDISON_H.description: Addison's disease, also known as primary adrenal insufficiency and hypocortisolism, is a long-term endocrine disorder in which the adrenal glands do not produce enough steroid hormones.

DIA_MOREDG_BLOC_ENZ_F.name: 21-OH Enzyme Block
DIA_MOREDG_BLOC_ENZ_F.description: Addison's disease, also known as primary adrenal insufficiency and hypocortisolism, is a long-term endocrine disorder in which the adrenal glands do not produce enough steroid hormones.

DIA_MOREDG_BLOC_ENZ_H.name: 21-OH Enzyme Block
DIA_MOREDG_BLOC_ENZ_H.description: Addison's disease, also known as primary adrenal insufficiency and hypocortisolism, is a long-term endocrine disorder in which the adrenal glands do not produce enough steroid hormones.

DIA_MOREDG_ICT.name: Corticotropic Insufficiency
DIA_MOREDG_ICT.description: Central adrenal insufficiency, or corticotropic insufficiency is from pituitary or hypothalamic causes or following the discontinuation of prolonged corticosteroid therapy (the most frequent cause). Aldosterone secretion is preserved, which explains a usually less severe picture.

DIA_MOREDG_HYPO_PT_AA.name: Autoimmune Hypoparathyroidism
DIA_MOREDG_HYPO_PT_AA.description: Hypoparathyroidism is a rare condition in which the parathyroid glands fail to produce sufficient amounts of parathyroid hormone or the parathyroid hormone produced lacks biologic activity. The parathyroid glands are part of the endocrine system, the network of glands that secrete hormones into the bloodstream where they travel to various areas of the body. These hormones regulate the chemical processes (metabolism) that influence the function of various organs and activities within the body.

DIA_MOREDG_HYPER_PT.name: Primary Hyperparathyroidism
DIA_MOREDG_HYPER_PT.description: Hyperparathyroidism is an increase in parathyroid hormone (PTH) levels in the blood. When this occurs from a disorder either within the parathyroid glands it's called primary hyperparathyroidism.

DIA_MOREDG_CONN.name: Conn's Syndrome
DIA_MOREDG_CONN.description: Primary aldosteronism, also known as primary hyperaldosteronism or Conn's syndrome, refers to the excess production of the hormone aldosterone from the adrenal glands, resulting in low renin levels. Conn's adenoma is a benign tumor of the adrenal cortex.

DIA_MOREDG_HBL_SRN.name: Bilateral Adrenal Hyperplasia
DIA_MOREDG_HBL_SRN.description: In bilateral hyperplasia, the excessive and uncontrolled production of aldosterone depends on the two adrenal glands.

DIA_MOREDG_INSULINOME.name: Insulinoma
DIA_MOREDG_INSULINOME.description: An insulinoma is a tumor of the pancreas that is derived from beta cells and secretes insulin. It is a rare form of a neuroendocrine tumor. Most insulinomas are benign in that they grow exclusively at their origin within the pancreas, but a minority metastasize.

DIA_MOREDG_SD_HGP.name: NIPHS
DIA_MOREDG_SD_HGP.description: Noninsulinoma pancreatogenous hypoglycemia syndrome (NIPHS) is a rare syndrome characterized by endogenous hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia that is not caused by an insulinoma.

DIA_MOREDG_QUERVAIN.name: De Quervain's Thyroiditis
DIA_MOREDG_QUERVAIN.description: De Quervain's thyroiditis, also known as subacute granulomatous thyroiditis or giant cell thyroiditis, is a member of the group of thyroiditis conditions known as resolving thyroiditis.

DIA_MOREDG_GOITRE_MULTINODULE.name: Toxic Multinodular Goiter
DIA_MOREDG_GOITRE_MULTINODULE.description: Toxic multinodular goiter is the natural evolution of ancient multinodular goiter.

DIA_MOREDG_ADENOME_TOXIQUE.name: Plummer's Toxic Adenoma
DIA_MOREDG_ADENOME_TOXIQUE.description: The toxic adenoma is a solitary, hyperfunctional benign tumor of the thyroid. This pathology indicates the presence of a localized hypertrophy from where the appearance of a protuberance of generally rounded shape. In addition, this type of adenoma is distinguished by its autonomous functioning.

TRM_MOREDG_ISRA.name: Adrenal Crisis
TRM_MOREDG_ISRA.description: Adrenal crisis is a potentially life-threatening medical condition requiring immediate emergency treatment. It is a constellation of symptoms that indicate severe adrenal insufficiency caused by insufficient levels of the hormone cortisol.

TRM_MOREDG_COMA_CETOACIDOSE.name: Diabetic Ketoacidosis
TRM_MOREDG_COMA_CETOACIDOSE.description: Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) is a potentially life-threatening complication of diabetes.

TRM_MOREDG_COMA_HYPEROSMOLAIRE.name: Hyperosmolar Hyperglycemic State
TRM_MOREDG_COMA_HYPEROSMOLAIRE.description: Hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state (HHS) is a complication of diabetes.

SYM_CRP_SEIZURES.name: Seizures
SYM_CRP_SEIZURES.description: A seizure is a sudden, uncontrolled electrical disturbance in the brain. It can cause changes in your behaviour, movements or feelings, and in levels of consciousness.

SYM_CRP_DYSPHASIA.name: Dysphasia
SYM_CRP_DYSPHASIA.description: Dysphasia is a language disorder marked by deficiency in the generation of speech, and sometimes also in its comprehension, due to brain disease or damage.

SYM_CRP_BREAST_DISCH.name: Breast Discharge
SYM_CRP_BREAST_DISCH.description: Nipple discharge is the release of fluid from the nipple. It is a very common breast symptom and in most cases is part of the normal function of the breast rather than being caused by a problem.

SYM_CRP_GYNECOMASTIA.name: Gynecomastia
SYM_CRP_GYNECOMASTIA.description: Abnormal increase in breast volume in men.

SYM_CRP_ED.name: Erectile Dysfunction
SYM_CRP_ED.description: Erectile dysfunction (ED) is the inability to get or keep an erection firm enough to have sexual intercourse. It's sometimes referred to as impotence, although this term is now used less often. Occasional ED isn't uncommon. Many men experience it during times of stress. Erectile dysfunction following radical prostatectomy for clinically localized prostate cancer is a known potential complication of the surgery.

SYM_CRP_IRR_MNS.name: Irregular Menstruation
SYM_CRP_IRR_MNS.description: Missed, delayed or erratic periods or abnormal bleeding patterns. Irregular menstruation can have causes that aren't due to underlying disease. Examples include family history of irregular menstruation, menopause or menarche, physical stress, psychological stress, smoking or medication side effects.

SYM_CRP_DYSPAREUNIA.name: Dyspareunia (Female)
SYM_CRP_DYSPAREUNIA.description: Painful intercourse can occur for reasons that range from structural problems to psychological concerns. Many women have painful intercourse at some point in their lives. The medical term for painful intercourse is dyspareunia, defined as persistent or recurrent genital pain that occurs just before, during or after intercourse.

SYM_CRP_AMENORRHEE.name: Amenorrhea
SYM_CRP_AMENORRHEE.description: Abnormal absence or suppression of menses.

SYM_CRP_SECHERESSE_VAGIN.name: Vaginal Dryness
SYM_CRP_SECHERESSE_VAGIN.description: Vaginal dryness is the result of decreased levels of estrogen.

SYM_CRP_NODULE_SKIN.name: Nodule On The Skin
SYM_CRP_NODULE_SKIN.description: A small, shiny bump or nodule on the skin

SYM_CRP_SEBORRHEE.name: Seborrhea
SYM_CRP_SEBORRHEE.description: Abnormally increased secretion and discharge of sebum.

SYM_CRP_HIRSUTISME.name: Hirsutism
SYM_CRP_HIRSUTISME.description: Excessive body hair in women on parts of the body where hair is normally absent or minimal.

SYM_CRP_BOUFFEE_VASOMOTRICE.name: Flushing
SYM_CRP_BOUFFEE_VASOMOTRICE.description: Flushing is to become markedly red in the face and often other areas of the skin, from various physiological conditions.

SYM_CRP_DRY_SKIN.name: Xerosis
SYM_CRP_DRY_SKIN.description: Dry skin is skin that has lost its protective fatty layer and becomes dehydrated.

SYM_CRP_TELANGIECTASIS.name: Telangiectasia
SYM_CRP_TELANGIECTASIS.description: Telangiectasias, also known as spider veins, are small dilated blood vessels that can occur near the surface of the skin or mucous membranes, measuring between 0.5 and 1 millimeter in diameter. These dilated blood vessels can develop anywhere on the body but are commonly seen on the face around the nose, cheeks and chin.

SYM_CRP_CHRONIC_WOUND.name: Chronic Wound
SYM_CRP_CHRONIC_WOUND.description: Chronic wounds are sores that don’t heal within about three months. They can start small and might scab over again and again, but they don’t get better.

SYM_CRP_FREQUENT_INFECT.name: Frequent Infections
SYM_CRP_FREQUENT_INFECT.description: Frequent infections are infections that are too great in number, too severe, or too long lasting. Frequent or recurrent infections can occur because the body isn’t producing enough white blood cells to keep the immune system working properly.

SYM_CRP_HYPONATREMIA.name: Hyponatremia
SYM_CRP_HYPONATREMIA.description: Hyponatremia occurs when the concentration of sodium in your blood is abnormally low. Sodium is an electrolyte, and it helps regulate the amount of water that's in and around your cells.

SYM_CRP_HYPERGLYCEMIA.name: Hyperglycemia
SYM_CRP_HYPERGLYCEMIA.description: Hyperglycemia is the technical term for high blood glucose (blood sugar). High blood sugar happens when the body has too little insulin or when the body can't use insulin properly.

SYM_CRP_HYPERCORTISOL.name: Hypercortisolism
SYM_CRP_HYPERCORTISOL.description: Cushing's syndrome, also called hypercortisolism, is a rare endocrine disorder caused by chronic exposure of the body's tissues to excess levels of cortisol - a hormone naturally produced by the adrenal gland.

SYM_CRP_HYPERALDOSTERONISM.name: Hyperaldosteronism
SYM_CRP_HYPERALDOSTERONISM.description: Hyperaldosteronism is a disease in which the adrenal gland(s) make too much aldosterone which leads to hypertension (high blood pressure) and low blood potassium levels. Primary hyperaldosteronism can be caused by either hyperactivity in one adrenal gland (unilateral disease) or both (bilateral disease).

SYM_CRP_THROMBOCYTOPENIA.name: Thrombocytopenia
SYM_CRP_THROMBOCYTOPENIA.description: Thrombocytopenia is a condition in which you have a low blood platelet count. Platelets (thrombocytes) are colourless blood cells that help blood clot. Platelets stop bleeding by clumping and forming plugs in blood vessel injuries.

SYM_CRP_RC_APLASIA.name: Red Cell Aplasia
SYM_CRP_RC_APLASIA.description: Pure red cell aplasia (PRCA) or erythroblastopenia refers to a type of anemia affecting the precursors to red blood cells but not to white blood cells. In PRCA, the bone marrow ceases to produce red blood cells. There are multiple etiologies that can cause PRCA.

SYM_CRP_PANCYTOPENIA.name: Pancytopenia
SYM_CRP_PANCYTOPENIA.description: Pancytopenia is a condition that occurs when a person has low counts for all three types of blood cells: red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets. Pancytopenia is usually due to a problem with the bone marrow that produces the blood cells. However, there can be several different underlying causes.

SYM_CRP_HYPOCALCEMIA.name: Hypocalcemia
SYM_CRP_HYPOCALCEMIA.description: Hypocalcemia is an abnormal drop in the level of calcium in the blood.

SYM_CRP_HYPERKALIEMIE.name: Hyperkalemia
SYM_CRP_HYPERKALIEMIE.description: Hyperkalemia is an elevated level of potassium (K+) in the blood.

SYM_CRP_HYPOKALIEMIE.name: Hypokalemia
SYM_CRP_HYPOKALIEMIE.description: Hypokalemia is a low level of potassium (K+) in the blood serum.

SYM_CRP_HYPERPRL.name: Hyperprolactinemia
SYM_CRP_HYPERPRL.description: Abnormal increase in plasma prolactin concentration.

SYM_CRP_HEMOLYSE.name: Haptoglobin - Low Levels
SYM_CRP_HEMOLYSE.description: Lab tests discovered descrese in level of Haptoglobin.

SYM_CRP_MED_APLASIA.name: Bone Aplasia
SYM_CRP_MED_APLASIA.description: Bone marrow aplasia refers to those hematologic conditions that are caused by a marked reduction and/or defect in the pluripotent or committed stem cells, or the failure of the bone marrow microenvironment to support hematopoiesis.

SYM_CRP_SURCHARGE_FER.name: Iron Overload
SYM_CRP_SURCHARGE_FER.description: Iron overload or hemochromatosis indicates accumulation of iron in the body from any cause.

SYM_CRP_HEPATOMEGALY.name: Hepatomegaly
SYM_CRP_HEPATOMEGALY.description: Hepatomegaly is when the patient has an abnormally large liver. It's often a sign of a serious health concern.

SYM_CRP_TURJESCENCE_JUGULAIRE.name: JVD
SYM_CRP_TURJESCENCE_JUGULAIRE.description: Jugular vein distention (JVD) occurs when the pressure inside the vena cava increases and appears as a bulge running down the right side of a person’s neck.

SYM_CRP_REFLUX_HJ.name: AJR
SYM_CRP_REFLUX_HJ.description: The abdominojugular reflux (AJR) sign is useful in diagnosing right ventricular failure, but is often performed and interpreted incorrectly.

SYM_CRP_ASCITES.name: Ascites
SYM_CRP_ASCITES.description: Abdominal swelling caused by accumulation of fluid, most often related to liver disease.

SYM_CRP_PLEURAL_EFFUSION.name: Pleural Effusion
SYM_CRP_PLEURAL_EFFUSION.description: Pleural effusion, sometimes referred to as “water on the lungs,” is the build-up of excess fluid between the layers of the pleura outside the lungs. The pleura are thin membranes that line the lungs and the inside of the chest cavity and act to lubricate and facilitate breathing.

SYM_CRP_DIFF_URINE.name: Urinary Retention
SYM_CRP_DIFF_URINE.description: Difficulty urinating and completely emptying the bladder. Urinary retention can have causes that aren't due to underlying disease. Examples include holding in urine intentionally or medication side effects.

SYM_CRP_OAP.name: Pulmonary Edema
SYM_CRP_OAP.description: Pulmonary edema is fluid accumulation in the tissue and air spaces of the lungs.

SYM_CRP_MARBRURE.name: Livedo Reticularis
SYM_CRP_MARBRURE.description: Livedo reticularis is a common skin finding consisting of a mottled reticulated vascular pattern that appears as a lace-like purplish discoloration of the skin. The discoloration is caused by swelling of the venules owing to obstruction of capillaries by small blood clots.

SYM_CRP_ENDOCARDITIS.name: Endocarditis Detected
SYM_CRP_ENDOCARDITIS.description: Echocardiography has discovered a vegatative growth on a valve in the heart, strongly suggestive of endocarditis.

SYM_CRP_DIABETES_SECONDARY.name: Diabetes
SYM_CRP_DIABETES_SECONDARY.description: Diabetes mellitus refers to a group of diseases that affect the metabolism of glucose. There are many types of diabetes, but generally there are too high levels of glucose in the blood.

SYM_CRP_CARPAL_TUNNEL.name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
SYM_CRP_CARPAL_TUNNEL.description: A condition where the median nerve in the wrist is compressed by the surrounding structures when passing through the tunnel near the base of the wrist. Pain and a sleeping sensation of the hand are the main symptoms.

SYM_CRP_ARF_FONCTION.name: Acute Renal Failure
SYM_CRP_ARF_FONCTION.description: Acute renal failure refers to an abrupt loss of kidney function, effectively seizing the ability to filter the blood and produce urine. The causes can be many, but are usually due to extensive tissue damage or decreased kidney blood flow.

SYM_CRP_ARF_ORGANIC.name: Acute Renal Failure
SYM_CRP_ARF_ORGANIC.description: Acute renal failure refers to an abrupt loss of kidney function, effectively seizing the ability to filter the blood and produce urine. The causes can be many, but are usually due to extensive tissue damage or decreased kidney blood flow.

SYM_CRP_HEART_ATTACK.name: Heart Attack
SYM_CRP_HEART_ATTACK.description: Electrocardiography has detected an ongoing heart attack.

SYM_CRP_STK.name: Brain Arteries Blocked
SYM_CRP_STK.description: Echocardiography has revealed the blockage of multiple arteries in the brain due to blood clots.

SYM_CRP_TAR.name: Traumatic Aortic Rupture
SYM_CRP_TAR.description: A patient suffered a traumatic rupture of the aorta, resulting in a massive internal hemorrhage.

SYM_CRP_THROMBOSIS.name: Superficial Thrombosis
SYM_CRP_THROMBOSIS.description: Ultrasonography has discovered superficial vein thrombosis.

SYM_CRP_GALLSTONES.name: Gallstones
SYM_CRP_GALLSTONES.description: Gallstones, also called cholelithiasis, are concretions of  usually made from bilirubin, bile acids and calcium formed in the gall bladder. Gallstones usually do not cause symptoms, however, if they block the bile duct they can cause a variety of serious conditions.

SYM_CRP_HTAP.name: Pulmonary Hypertension Detected
SYM_CRP_HTAP.description: Echocardiography has revealed pulmonary hypertension (hypertension in the arteries of the lungs).

SYM_CRP_HEMORRAGIE_DIG_HAUTE.name: UDH
SYM_CRP_HEMORRAGIE_DIG_HAUTE.description: During an upper digestive hemorrhage (UDH), the bleeding occurs upstream of the Treitz ligament (oesophagus, stomach and duodenum).

SYM_CRP_HEMORRAGIE_DIG_BASSE.name: LDH
SYM_CRP_HEMORRAGIE_DIG_BASSE.description: In a lower digestive hemorrhage (LDH), blood loss originates downstream from the Treitz ligament (small intestine, colon, rectum and anus).

SYM_CRP_SUPRACLAVICULAR_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_SUPRACLAVICULAR_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_AXILLARY_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_AXILLARY_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_SOUFFLE_ABDOMINAL.name: Abdominal Murmur
SYM_CRP_SOUFFLE_ABDOMINAL.description: The presence of an abdominal murmur may reflect the existence of an aneurysm of the abdominal aorta or a narrowing of the renal arteries.

SYM_CRP_POUL_HYPERPULSE.name: Bounding Pulse
SYM_CRP_POUL_HYPERPULSE.description: A bounding pulse signifies high pulse pressure. It may be due to low peripheral resistance, increased cardiac output, increased stroke volume or decreased distensibility of the arterial system.

SYM_CRP_ABOLITION_POULS.name: No Pulse
SYM_CRP_ABOLITION_POULS.description: There is no perceived pulse to a member.

SYM_MOREDG_HYPERCALCEMIA.name: Hypercalcemia
SYM_MOREDG_HYPERCALCEMIA.description: Hypercalcemia is a condition in which the calcium level in your blood is above normal. Hypercalcaemia is most often caused by overactivity in the four tiny glands in the neck (parathyroid glands) or from cancer. Extra calcium in the blood affects many bodily systems.

SYM_MOREDG_SEVERE_HYPERCALCEMIA.name: Severe Hypercalcemia
SYM_MOREDG_SEVERE_HYPERCALCEMIA.description: Severe hypercalcemia is a medical emergency requiring urgent treatment. Hypercalcemia is a condition in which the calcium level in your blood is above normal. Rarely, severe hypercalcemia can interfere with your heart function, causing palpitations and fainting, indications of cardiac arrhythmia, and other heart problems.

SYM_MOREDG_SPLENOMEGALY.name: Splenomegaly
SYM_MOREDG_SPLENOMEGALY.description: Splenomegaly is a condition that occurs when your spleen becomes enlarged. It’s also commonly referred to as enlarged spleen or spleen enlargement.

SYM_MOREDG_ABCES_CEREBRAL.name: Brain Abscess
SYM_MOREDG_ABCES_CEREBRAL.description: Brain abscess (or cerebral abscess) is an abscess caused by inflammation and collection of infected material, coming from local (ear infection, dental abscess, infection of paranasal sinuses, infection of the mastoid air cells of the temporal bone, epidural abscess) or remote (lung, heart, kidney etc.) infectious sources, within the brain tissue.

SYM_MOREDG_SOUFFLE_SYSTOLIQUE.name: Systolic Heart Murmurs
SYM_MOREDG_SOUFFLE_SYSTOLIQUE.description: Systolic heart murmurs are heart murmurs heard during systole. The systole is the part of the cardiac cycle during which some chambers of the heart muscle contract after refilling with blood.

SYM_MOREDG_HVG.name: LVH
SYM_MOREDG_HVG.description: Left ventricular hypertrophy (LVH) is thickening of the heart muscle of the left ventricle of the heart, that is, left-sided ventricular hypertrophy.

SYM_MOREDG_SOUFFLE_DIASTOLIQUE.name: Diastolic Heart Murmurs
SYM_MOREDG_SOUFFLE_DIASTOLIQUE.description: Diastolic heart murmurs are heart murmurs heard during diastole. Diastole is the part of the cardiac cycle during which the heart refills with blood after the emptying done during systole (contraction).

SYM_MOREDG_S1Q3.name: S1Q3
SYM_MOREDG_S1Q3.description: Abbreviation which means “S wave in DI and Q wave in DIII” at the ECG. It translates a dextrorotation - physiological or pathological - along the longitudinal axis of the heart, which brings the right ventricle further forward and to the right of the left ventricle. This sign, alone or in combination with other ECG signs, is sometimes used to reinforce a hypothesis of pulmonary embolism.

SYM_MOREDG_SCORE_WELLS.name: Wells Score Positive
SYM_MOREDG_SCORE_WELLS.description: This is a diagnostic score for pulmonary embolism.

SYM_MOREDG_SCORE_GENEVE.name: Positive Geneva Score
SYM_MOREDG_SCORE_GENEVE.description: This is a diagnostic score for pulmonary embolism and deep vein thrombosis.

SYM_MOREDG_DDIMERE.name: High D-Dimer
SYM_MOREDG_DDIMERE.description: D-dimers are produced by the breakdown of fibrin, a protein involved in blood clotting. The doctor prescribes a dosage of D-dimers if he suspects the presence of blood clots.

SYM_MOREDG_BNP.name: High BNP
SYM_MOREDG_BNP.description: BNP (Brain natriuretic peptide), is a neuro-hormone synthesized by heart cells called cardiomyocytes in response to an increase in blood pressure, especially during the development of heart failure.

SYM_MOREDG_MASSE_HYPERBATTANTE.name: Para-Umbilical Mass
SYM_MOREDG_MASSE_HYPERBATTANTE.description: Perception of a swinging and expansive para-umbilical mass.

SYM_MOREDG_SYNDROME_LOGE.name: Compartment Syndrome
SYM_MOREDG_SYNDROME_LOGE.description: Compartment syndrome is a condition in which increased pressure within one of the body's anatomical compartments results in insufficient blood supply to tissue within that space. There are two main types: acute and chronic. Compartments of the leg or arm are most commonly involved.

SYM_MOREDG_INFARCTUS_MESENTERIQUE.name: Mesenteric Ischemia
SYM_MOREDG_INFARCTUS_MESENTERIQUE.description: Mesenteric ischemia is a medical condition in which injury to the small intestine occurs due to not enough blood supply. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at General Surgery dpt + ICU

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_HYPOPHYSE.name: Pituitary Adenoma
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_HYPOPHYSE.description: Discovery of a pituitary adenoma on MRI. This doesn't provide information on the secreting nature or not of it.

SYM_MOREDG_RETRECISSEMENT_AORTIQUE.name: Aortic Stenosis
SYM_MOREDG_RETRECISSEMENT_AORTIQUE.description: Decrease in diameter of the aortic valve.

SYM_MOREDG_SYNCOPE_REFLEXE.name: Reflex Syncope
SYM_MOREDG_SYNCOPE_REFLEXE.description: It is a neurovegetative reflex of arterial hypotension with more or less marked bradycardia following a stimulus.

SYM_MOREDG_SYNCOPE_SA.name: Conduction Disorder
SYM_MOREDG_SYNCOPE_SA.description: Complete paroxysmal blockage of the intracardiac conduction.

SYM_MOREDG_LIPOTHIMIE.name: Prodromes
SYM_MOREDG_LIPOTHIMIE.description: The signs and symptoms that precede syncope are called prodromes. Lipothymia can be summed up as an imminent feeling of fainting.

SYM_MOREDG_FA_ECG.name: AF
SYM_MOREDG_FA_ECG.description: The diagnosis of AF can only be confirmed on an ECG trace. This shows an absence of visible P wave and the most often irregular QRS.

SYM_MOREDG_BSA_ECG.name: SAB
SYM_MOREDG_BSA_ECG.description: Sino-Atrial Block (SAB) is a complete interruption of intra-atrial conduction after certain sinus impulses.

SYM_MOREDG_BAV3_ECG.name: AVB3
SYM_MOREDG_BAV3_ECG.description: The 3rd degree Atrioventricular Block is a complete and permanent interruption of the transmission of atrial impulses to the ventricles..

SYM_MOREDG_BBG_ECG.name: LBBB
SYM_MOREDG_BBG_ECG.description: Typical appearance of LBBB at ECG.

SYM_MOREDG_TV_ECG.name: VT
SYM_MOREDG_TV_ECG.description: Typical appearance of VT at ECG.

SYM_MOREDG_QT_LONG.name: QT Extension
SYM_MOREDG_QT_LONG.description: Extension of the QT space at the ECG.

SYM_MOREDG_TCJ_ECG.name: JT
SYM_MOREDG_TCJ_ECG.description: Junctional Tachycardia (JT) is a heterogeneous group of tachycardias whose circuit begins or necessarily passes through the atrioventricular junction.

SYM_MOREDG_ONDE_DELTA.name: Delta Wave
SYM_MOREDG_ONDE_DELTA.description: Impasto and slowing of the initial part of the QRS complex in connection with pre-excitation of the ventricle by an accessory bundle.

SYM_MOREDG_INSUFFISANCE_CARDIAQUE_G.name: Altered LVEF
SYM_MOREDG_INSUFFISANCE_CARDIAQUE_G.description: The Left Ventricle Ejection Fraction (LVEF) is the percentage of blood ejected from the left ventricle.

SYM_MOREDG_INSUFFISANCE_CARDIAQUE_D.name: Altered RVEF
SYM_MOREDG_INSUFFISANCE_CARDIAQUE_D.description: The Right Ventricle Ejection Fraction (RVEF) is the percentage of blood ejected from the right ventricle.

SYM_MOREDG_IPS_BAS.name: Low SPI
SYM_MOREDG_IPS_BAS.description: Systolic Pressure Index (SPI) is less than 0.9. IPS is based on the collection of systolic pressures from the lower and humeral limbs and the establishment of an ankle pressure / humeral pressure ratio.

SYM_MOREDG_OCCLUSION_A_COELIAQUE.name: Celiac Occlusion
SYM_MOREDG_OCCLUSION_A_COELIAQUE.description: Occlusion of the celiac artery and mesenteric arteries.

SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MS.name: Upper Limb Tissue Ischemia
SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MS.description: Ischemia is the cessation or insufficiency of blood circulation in a part of the body or an organ, which deprives the cells of oxygen supply and causes their necrosis. Ischemia can be due to blockage of a vessel (thrombosis) or compression of an artery (stenosis).

SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MI.name: Lower Limb Tissue Ischemia
SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MI.description: Ischemia is the cessation or insufficiency of blood circulation in a part of the body or an organ, which deprives the cells of oxygen supply and causes their necrosis. Ischemia can be due to blockage of a vessel (thrombosis) or compression of an artery (stenosis).

SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MESENTERIQUE.name: Occlusion Of Mesenteric Arteries
SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MESENTERIQUE.description: It is an acute arterial occlusion or low flow.

SYM_MOREDG_RUPTURE_ANEVRISME_AIC.name: IAA Ruptured
SYM_MOREDG_RUPTURE_ANEVRISME_AIC.description: Rupture of a common iliac artery aneurysm.

SYM_MOREDG_TRANSPLANTATION_COEUR.name: Transplant
SYM_MOREDG_TRANSPLANTATION_COEUR.description: This patient urgently needs a heart transplant!

SYM_MOREDG_PROLACTINOME_F.name: Prolactinome
SYM_MOREDG_PROLACTINOME_F.description: Discovery of a prolactinoma on MRI.

SYM_MOREDG_PROLACTINOME_H.name: Prolactinome
SYM_MOREDG_PROLACTINOME_H.description: Discovery of a prolactinoma on MRI.

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_SOMATOTROPE.name: Somatotropic Adenoma
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_SOMATOTROPE.description: No braking during the "minute" brake test.

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_CORTICOTROPE_F.name: Adenome Corticotrope
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_CORTICOTROPE_F.description: The test is in favor of the diagnosis of non-curable hypercortisolism if the cortisolemia does not drop below the threshold of 18 ng / ml [50 nmol / l]

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_CORTICOTROPE_H.name: Adenome Corticotrope
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_CORTICOTROPE_H.description: The test is in favor of the diagnosis of non-curable hypercortisolism if the cortisolemia does not drop below the threshold of 18 ng / ml [50 nmol / l]

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_THYREOTROPE.name: Thyrotropic Adenoma
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_THYREOTROPE.description: Discovery of central hyperthyroidism at the blood test. This is characterized by a high concentration of thyroid hormones associated with a normal high or abnormal concentration of TSH.

SYM_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPITUITARISME_F.name: Pan Hypopituitarisme
SYM_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPITUITARISME_F.description: Discovery of a pituitary adenoma that prevents the proper functioning of the pineal gland on MRI.

SYM_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPITUITARISME_H.name: Pan Hypopituitarisme
SYM_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPITUITARISME_H.description: Discovery of a pituitary adenoma that prevents the proper functioning of the pineal gland on MRI.

SYM_MOREDG_ISRNP_F.name: Isrnp
SYM_MOREDG_ISRNP_F.description: Discovery of peripheral adrenal insufficiency during the Synacthene test. This is characterized by a low cortisol level associated with a high concentration of ACTH.

SYM_MOREDG_ISRNP_H.name: Isrnp
SYM_MOREDG_ISRNP_H.description: Discovery of peripheral adrenal insufficiency during the Synacthene test. This is characterized by a low cortisol level associated with a high concentration of ACTH.

SYM_MOREDG_21OH_F.name: 21oh
SYM_MOREDG_21OH_F.description: It is accepted by the entire endocrinologist community that the diagnosis of D21OH-NC is based on the dosage of 17OHP in the morning and at best in the first part of the cycle. The sample must be taken in the absence of or at a distance from treatment with glucocorticoids, since even "substitute" treatment and a fortiori pharmacological treatment introduces a risk of ignoring the diagnosis (false negative) by inhibiting the corticotropic axis. The sampling for the determination of 17OHP must also be done in the morning, because if it is done in the afternoon, the concentration of 17OHP can be found within the normal range in a patient with D21OH-NC simply because of fluctuations nycthemeral of adrenal cortical function.

SYM_MOREDG_21OH_H.name: 21oh
SYM_MOREDG_21OH_H.description: It is accepted by the entire endocrinologist community that the diagnosis of D21OH-NC is based on the dosage of 17OHP in the morning and at best in the first part of the cycle. The sample must be taken in the absence of or at a distance from treatment with glucocorticoids, since even "substitute" treatment and a fortiori pharmacological treatment introduces a risk of ignoring the diagnosis (false negative) by inhibiting the corticotropic axis. The sampling for the determination of 17OHP must also be done in the morning, because if it is done in the afternoon, the concentration of 17OHP can be found within the normal range in a patient with D21OH-NC simply because of fluctuations nycthemeral of adrenal cortical function.

SYM_MOREDG_ISF_CORTICOT.name: Central Adrenal Insufficiency
SYM_MOREDG_ISF_CORTICOT.description: Discovery of central adrenal insufficiency during the Synacthene test. This is characterized by low cortisol levels associated with a low or abnormally normal concentration of ACTH.

SYM_MOREDG_AC_ANTI_CASR.name: Anti-CASR Ab
SYM_MOREDG_AC_ANTI_CASR.description: Discovery of anti-CASR antibody during the ELISA test.

SYM_MOREDG_CONN.name: Adrenal Adenoma
SYM_MOREDG_CONN.description: Discovery of an adrenal adenoma secreting on CT.

SYM_MOREDG_HPBL_SRN.name: Adrenal Hypertrophy
SYM_MOREDG_HPBL_SRN.description: Increase in size of the adrenal gland on CT scan.

SYM_MOREDG_INSULINOME.name: Inappropriate Secretion
SYM_MOREDG_INSULINOME.description: Inappropriate (normal or high) insulin and peptide C secretion in hypoglycaemia.

SYM_MOREDG_SD_HGP.name: Pancreatic Hypoglycemia
SYM_MOREDG_SD_HGP.description: Hypoglycemia of pancreatic origin not linked to an insulin-secreting adenoma.

SYM_MOREDG_GOITRE_MULTINODULAIRE_TOXIQUE.name: "Checkerboard" Scintigraphy
SYM_MOREDG_GOITRE_MULTINODULAIRE_TOXIQUE.description: Alternating hot and cold beaches (checkerboard) with scintigraphy.

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_TOXIQUE.name: Toxic Adenoma
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_TOXIQUE.description: Hyperfixation of the isotope at the level of the nodule while the rest of the parenchyma is hypofixant or cold or "extinct" (due to the decrease in TSH), on scintigraphy.

SYM_MOREDG_CORTISOL_EFF.name: Very Low Cortisol
SYM_MOREDG_CORTISOL_EFF.description: The cortisol level in the blood is very low.

SYM_MOREDG_CETONEMIE.name: Ketonemia
SYM_MOREDG_CETONEMIE.description: An excess of ketones in the blood.

SYM_MOREDG_PBG-D.name: Low PBG-D
SYM_MOREDG_PBG-D.description: Porphobilinogen deaminase (hydroxymethylbilane synthase, or uroporphyrinogen I synthase) is an enzyme that in humans is encoded by the HMBS gene. Porphobilinogen deaminase is involved in the third step of the heme biosynthetic pathway.

SYM_MOREDG_SPHEROCYTE.name: Spherocyte
SYM_MOREDG_SPHEROCYTE.description: Discovery of spherocytes in the blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_GPI.name: Low GPI
SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_GPI.description: Glucose-6-phosphate isomerase (GPI), alternatively known as phosphoglucose isomerase/phosphoglucoisomerase (PGI) or phosphohexose isomerase (PHI), is an enzyme that in humans is encoded by the GPI gene on chromosome 19. In the cytoplasm, the gene product functions as a glycolytic enzyme (glucose-6-phosphate isomerase) that interconverts glucose-6-phosphate (G6P) and fructose-6-phosphate (F6P).

SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_G6PD.name: Low G6PD
SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_G6PD.description: Glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD or G6PDH) is a cytosolic enzyme that helps protect the red blood cells against oxidative damage from compounds like hydrogen peroxide.

SYM_MOREDG_PK_BAS.name: Low PK
SYM_MOREDG_PK_BAS.description: Pyruvate kinase is the enzyme involved in the last step of glycolysis.

SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_ADAMTS13.name: Low ADAMTS13
SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_ADAMTS13.description: ADAMTS13 (a disintegrin and metalloproteinase with a thrombospondin type 1 motif, member 13)—also known as von Willebrand factor-cleaving protease (VWFCP)—is a zinc-containing metalloprotease enzyme that cleaves von Willebrand factor (vWf), a large protein involved in blood clotting.

SYM_MOREDG_DREPANOCYTE_A.name: Drepanocyte
SYM_MOREDG_DREPANOCYTE_A.description: Discovery of drepanocytes in the blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_DREPANOCYTE_C.name: Drepanocyte
SYM_MOREDG_DREPANOCYTE_C.description: Discovery of drepanocytes in the blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_vWF.name: Low VWF
SYM_MOREDG_vWF.description: von Willebrand factor (VWF) (/ˌfʌnˈvɪlɪbrɑːnt/) is a blood glycoprotein involved in hemostasis.

SYM_MOREDG_NPC1_MUTE.name: NPC1 Muté
SYM_MOREDG_NPC1_MUTE.description: The proteins NPC1 and NPC2 seem to work at the level of the endo-lysosomal system to facilitate the intracellular transport of cholesterol and other molecules, but their precise function is not yet known.

SYM_MOREDG_PLAQUETTES_GEANTES.name: Macrothrombocytes
SYM_MOREDG_PLAQUETTES_GEANTES.description: More than 10% of the platelets studied are giant.

SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_AIIB_B3.name: Abnormal Integrin
SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_AIIB_B3.description: Quantitative and / or qualitative anomalies of the integrin alphaIIb beta3 are found.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_RP.name: RP Mutations
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_RP.description: Ribosomal proteins (RPs) from either the small (RPS7, RPS17, RPS19, RPS24) or the large (RPL5, RPL11, RPL35a) ribosomal subunit are mutated.

SYM_MOREDG_GLUCOCEREBROSIDASE.name: Glucocerebrosidase Deficiency
SYM_MOREDG_GLUCOCEREBROSIDASE.description: Low glucocerebrosidase level leading to accumulation of glucosylceramide deposits.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_SH2D1A.name: SH2D1A Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_SH2D1A.description: Mutation of the SH2D1A gene which is located on the X chromosome. This gene codes for a protein called SAP (signalling lymphocyte activation molecular [SLAM] associated protein).

SYM_MOREDG_SIDEROBLASTE.name: Sideroblast
SYM_MOREDG_SIDEROBLASTE.description: Sideroblasts have been found. These are iron-containing granulations stained with Perls's reagent, corresponding to aggregates of ribosomes and ferritin.

SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_C.name: Hemoglobin  C
SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_C.description: Discovery of hemoglobins type C with a blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_D.name: Hemoglobin  D
SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_D.description: Discovery of hemoglobins type D with a blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_E.name: Hemoglobin  E
SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_E.description: Discovery of hemoglobins type E with a blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_M.name: Hemoglobin  M
SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_M.description: Discovery of hemoglobins type M with a blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_TRIADE_PLUMMER_VINSON.name: Triad
SYM_MOREDG_TRIADE_PLUMMER_VINSON.description: Classical triad of dysphagia, iron-deficiency anemia and esophageal webs.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_ACVRL_ENG.name: Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_ACVRL_ENG.description: A mutation in one of the two ACVRL or ENG genes involved in the TGF-beta signaling pathway has been found.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_HFE.name: HFE Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_HFE.description: The HFE gene located on chromosome 6 is mutated. Two types of alterations exist: the C282Y mutation and the H63D mutation.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_HJV_OU_HAMP.name: Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_HJV_OU_HAMP.description: Mutations in the gene coding for hemojuvelin (HJV) on chromosome 1 or mutations in the gene coding for hepcidin (HAMP) on chromosome 19 have been found.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_TFR2.name: TFR2 Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_TFR2.description: Mutations in the transferrin receptor-2 (TFR2) gene on chromosome 7 have been found.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_SLC40A1.name: SLC40A1 Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_SLC40A1.description: Mutations in the SLC40A1 gene located on chromosome 2 which codes for ferroportin (FPN) have been found.

SYM_MOREDG_MO_HYPOCELLULAIRE.name: Hypocellular Bone Marrow
SYM_MOREDG_MO_HYPOCELLULAIRE.description: The bone marrow is hypocellular, it's "empty".

SYM_MOREDG_ANTICORPS_ANTI_F4P.name: Anti-PF4 Ab
SYM_MOREDG_ANTICORPS_ANTI_F4P.description: Anti-PF4 / heparin antibodies were detected by ELISA.

EXM_CRP_LIVER_FT_SAMPLING.name: Liver Function Test Sampling
EXM_CRP_LIVER_FT_SAMPLING.description: Liver function tests (also called LFTs) are blood tests that can provide information about how your liver is working. They test the levels of a number of proteins and enzymes that are either produced by liver cells or released into the blood when liver cells are damaged. \n\nRequired Lab: Haematology

EXM_CRP_MINERALT_SAMPLE.name: Comprehensive Mineral Panel Sampling
EXM_CRP_MINERALT_SAMPLE.description: The Comprehensive Mineral Panel includes testing the blood for five minerals and is used to detect deficiencies or overexposure (toxicity) across the mineral spectrum. This panel includes testing levels of the following five minerals: Calcium, Copper, Magnesium, Manganese and Zinc. \n\nRequired Lab: Haematology

EXM_CRP_BLOOD_SMEAR_SAMPLE.name: Blood Smear Sample
EXM_CRP_BLOOD_SMEAR_SAMPLE.description: A blood film—or peripheral blood smear—is a thin layer of blood smeared on a glass microscope slide and then stained in such a way as to allow the various blood cells to be examined microscopically. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_COLONOSCOPY.name: Colonoscopy
EXM_CRP_COLONOSCOPY.description: A colonoscopy is a medical procedure that is done to look for cancer, colon polyps or other abnormalities inside your bowel. Colonoscopy is done using an instrument called a colonoscope (a type of endoscope) — a long, thin, flexible tube containing a camera and a light.

EXM_CRP_PELVIC_EXAM.name: Pelvic Exam
EXM_CRP_PELVIC_EXAM.description: A pelvic exam is a doctor's visual and physical examination of a woman's reproductive organs. During the exam, the doctor inspects the vagina, cervix, fallopian tubes, vulva, ovaries, and uterus.

EXM_CRP_SEIN.name: Breast Examination
EXM_CRP_SEIN.description: Visual inspection and palpation of breast.

EXM_CRP_SCINTIGRAPHIE.name: Scintigraphy
EXM_CRP_SCINTIGRAPHIE.description: Scintigraphy (from Latin scintilla, "spark"), also known as a gamma scan, is a diagnostic test in nuclear medicine, where radioisotopes attached to drugs that travel to a specific organ or tissue (radiopharmaceuticals) are taken internally and the emitted gamma radiation is captured by external detectors (gamma cameras) to form two-dimensional images in a similar process to the capture of x-ray images.

EXM_CRP_FLOWCYT.name: Flow Cytometry Sample
EXM_CRP_FLOWCYT.description: Flow cytometry is a technique used to detect and measure physical and chemical characteristics of a population of cells or particles. A sample containing cells or particles is suspended in a fluid and injected into the flow cytometer instrument. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_FISH_SAMPLE.name: F.I.S.H. Sampling
EXM_CRP_FISH_SAMPLE.description: Fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) is a molecular cytogenetic technique that uses fluorescent probes that bind to only those parts of a nucleic acid sequence with a high degree of sequence complementarity. It was developed by biomedical researchers in the early 1980s to detect and localize the presence or absence of specific DNA sequences on chromosomes. Fluorescence microscopy can be used to find out where the fluorescent probe is bound to the chromosomes. FISH is often used for finding specific features in DNA for use in genetic counseling, medicine, and species identification. FISH can also be used to detect and localize specific RNA targets (mRNA, lncRNA and miRNA) in cells, circulating tumor cells, and tissue samples. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_BONEBIOPSY_SAMPLE.name: Bone Marrow Biopsy Sample
EXM_CRP_BONEBIOPSY_SAMPLE.description: A bone marrow biopsy collects a cylindrical core sample that preserves the marrow's structure. The biopsy sample is evaluated by a specialist to determine cellularity, the volume of cells is compared to the volume of other components of the bone marrow, whether the different cell lineages are present in adequate numbers and if there are any abnormal infiltrates in the marrow. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_ELISA_SAMPLING.name: ELISA
EXM_CRP_ELISA_SAMPLING.description: The enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) is a commonly used analytical biochemistry assay. The assay uses a solid-phase enzyme immunoassay (EIA) to detect the presence of a ligand (commonly a protein) in a liquid sample using antibodies directed against the protein to be measured. ELISA has been used as a diagnostic tool in medicine, plant pathology, and biotechnology, as well as a quality control check in various industries. In the most simple form of an ELISA, antigens from the sample are attached to a surface. Then, a matching antibody is applied over the surface so it can bind to the antigen. This antibody is linked to an enzyme, and in the final step, a substance containing the enzyme's substrate is added. The subsequent reaction produces a detectable signal, most commonly a color change.

EXM_MOREDG_FREIN_SAMPLING.name: Dexamethasone Suppression Test
EXM_MOREDG_FREIN_SAMPLING.description: The dexamethasone suppression test (DST) is used to assess adrenal gland function by measuring how cortisol levels change in response to an injection of dexamethasone. It is typically used to diagnose Cushing's syndrome.

EXM_MOREDG_FREIN_TESTING.name: Dexamethasone Suppression Test - Testing
EXM_MOREDG_FREIN_TESTING.description: The dexamethasone suppression test (DST) is used to assess adrenal gland function by measuring how cortisol levels change in response to an injection of dexamethasone. It is typically used to diagnose Cushing's syndrome.

EXM_MOREDG_SYNACTHENE_SAMPLING.name: ACTH Stimulation Test
EXM_MOREDG_SYNACTHENE_SAMPLING.description: The ACTH test (also called the cosyntropin, tetracosactide, or Synacthen test) is a medical test usually ordered and interpreted by endocrinologists to assess the functioning of the adrenal glands's stress response by measuring the adrenal response to adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH; corticotropin) or another corticotropic agent such as tetracosactide (cosyntropin, tetracosactrin; Synacthen) or alsactide (Synchrodyn). ACTH is a hormone produced in the anterior pituitary gland that stimulates the adrenal glands to release cortisol, dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA), dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate (DHEA-S), and aldosterone. During the test, a small amount of synthetic ACTH is injected, and the amount of cortisol (and sometimes aldosterone) that the adrenals produce in response is measured. This test may cause mild side effects in some individuals.

EXM_MOREDG_SYNACTHENE_TESTING.name: ACTH Stimulation Test - Testing
EXM_MOREDG_SYNACTHENE_TESTING.description: The ACTH test (also called the cosyntropin, tetracosactide, or Synacthen test) is a medical test usually ordered and interpreted by endocrinologists to assess the functioning of the adrenal glands's stress response by measuring the adrenal response to adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH; corticotropin) or another corticotropic agent such as tetracosactide (cosyntropin, tetracosactrin; Synacthen) or alsactide (Synchrodyn). ACTH is a hormone produced in the anterior pituitary gland that stimulates the adrenal glands to release cortisol, dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA), dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate (DHEA-S), and aldosterone. During the test, a small amount of synthetic ACTH is injected, and the amount of cortisol (and sometimes aldosterone) that the adrenals produce in response is measured. This test may cause mild side effects in some individuals.

EXM_MOREDG_TEST_COMBS_SAMPLING.name: Coombs Test
EXM_MOREDG_TEST_COMBS_SAMPLING.description: A Coombs test, also known as antiglobulin test (AGT) is either of two blood tests used in immunohematology. They are the direct and indirect Coombs tests. The direct Coombs test detects antibodies that are stuck to the surface of the red blood cells.

EXM_MOREDG_TEST_COMBS_TESTING.name: Coombs Test - Testing
EXM_MOREDG_TEST_COMBS_TESTING.description: A Coombs test, also known as antiglobulin test (AGT) is either of two blood tests used in immunohematology. They are the direct and indirect Coombs tests. The direct Coombs test detects antibodies that are stuck to the surface of the red blood cells.

TRT_CRP_ESTRADIOL_CREAM.name: Estradiol Cream
TRT_CRP_ESTRADIOL_CREAM.description: Estradiol cream is a preparation of estrogen, a female hormone, for topical application to the vaginal area used to treat certain symptoms of menopause such as dryness, burning, and itching of the vaginal area and urgency or irritation with urination. \n\nRequired room: Any office.

TRT_CRP_ANTI_ACNEE.name: Anti-Acneic
TRT_CRP_ANTI_ACNEE.description: Medicine used to treat various types and forms of acne.

TRT_CRP_MOISTURIZER_CREAM.name: Moisturizer Cream
TRT_CRP_MOISTURIZER_CREAM.description: A cosmetic preparation used to prevent dryness in the skin.

TRT_CRP_MITOTANE.name: Mitotane
TRT_CRP_MITOTANE.description: Mitotane, sold under the brand name Lysodren, is a steroidogenesis inhibitor and cytostatic antineoplastic medication which is used in the treatment of adrenocortical carcinoma and Cushing's syndrome. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_SPIRONOLACTONE.name: Spironolactone
TRT_CRP_SPIRONOLACTONE.description: Spironolactone is a potassium-sparing diuretic (water pill) that prevents your body from absorbing too much salt and keeps your potassium levels from getting too low. Spironolactone is used to treat heart failure, high blood pressure (hypertension), or hypokalemia (low potassium levels in the blood). \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_HORMONE_THERAPY.name: Hormone Therapy
TRT_CRP_HORMONE_THERAPY.description: Hormone therapy or hormonal therapy is the use of hormones in medical treatment. \n\nRequired room: Any office.

TRT_CRP_ANTI_ANDROGEN.name: Anti-Androgens
TRT_CRP_ANTI_ANDROGEN.description: Antiandrogen agents block androgen receptors, thereby inhibiting the effects of male sex hormones.

TRT_CRP_PROGESTATIF.name: Progestogen
TRT_CRP_PROGESTATIF.description: A naturally occurring or synthetic progestational steroid.

TRT_CRP_OESTROGENOTHERAPIE.name: Estrogen Replacement Therapy
TRT_CRP_OESTROGENOTHERAPIE.description: Hormone replacement therapy involving the administration of estrogen without progestin.

TRT_CRP_CHELATEUR_FER.name: Iron Chelators
TRT_CRP_CHELATEUR_FER.description: The goal of iron chelation therapy is to prevent iron-mediated injury to cells.

TRT_CRP_AUTOGRAFT.name: Autograft
TRT_CRP_AUTOGRAFT.description: A graft of tissue from one point to another of the same individual's body.

TRT_CRP_ALLOGRAFT.name: Allograft
TRT_CRP_ALLOGRAFT.description: A tissue graft from a donor of the same species as the recipient but not genetically identical.

TRT_CRP_KCL.name: Potassium Salt
TRT_CRP_KCL.description: The most used is potassium chloride in micro-encapsulated form. It is an oral potassium supplement

TRT_CRP_HYDROCORTISONE.name: Hydrocortisone
TRT_CRP_HYDROCORTISONE.description: Hydrocortisone is the name for the hormone cortisol when supplied as a medication.

TRT_CRP_SCLEROTHERAPIE.name: Sclerotherapy
TRT_CRP_SCLEROTHERAPIE.description: It's the treatment of varicose blood vessels by the injection of an irritant which causes inflammation, coagulation of blood, and narrowing of the blood vessel wall.

TRT_CRP_IPP_IV.name: IV PPI
TRT_CRP_IPP_IV.description: Treatment with a high dose Proton Pump Inhibitor before gastroscopy decreases the need for endoscopic hemostasis.

TRT_CRP_INCISION_AND_DRAINAGE.name: Incision And Drainage
TRT_CRP_INCISION_AND_DRAINAGE.description: Incision and drainage and clinical lancing are minor surgical procedures to release fuild or pressure built up under the skin. \n\nRequired room: Oncology Diagnostic Unit / Oncology Doctor's Office / ICU / Observation Room

TRT_CRP_RADIO_EMBOLISATION.name: Endovascular Embolization
TRT_CRP_RADIO_EMBOLISATION.description: In endovascular embolization, a catheter is inserts into a leg artery and threads it through blood vessels to injects an embolizing agent using X-ray imaging.

TRT_MOREDG_ANDROGENOTHERAPIE.name: Androgen Replacement Therapy
TRT_MOREDG_ANDROGENOTHERAPIE.description: Androgen replacement therapy (ART), often referred to as testosterone replacement therapy (TRT), is a form of hormone therapy in which androgens, often testosterone, are replaced. ART is often prescribed to counter the effects of male hypogonadism.

TRT_MOREDG_AGONISTE_DOPA.name: Dopaminergic Agonist
TRT_MOREDG_AGONISTE_DOPA.description: A dopamine receptor agonist (DA) is a compound that activates dopamine receptors. There are two families of dopamine receptors, D2-like and D1-like, and they are all G protein-coupled receptors. D1- and D5-receptors belong to the D1-like family and the D2-like family includes D2, D3 and D4 receptors. The DA are used in Parkinson’s disease and, to a lesser extent, to treat depression, hyperprolactinemia and restless leg syndrome.

TRT_MOREDG_SUPPLEMENT_vitD.name: Vitamin-D Supplement
TRT_MOREDG_SUPPLEMENT_vitD.description: Supplementation of vitamin D3. \n\nRequired room: Office / Diagnostic unit at Orthopaedy dpt.

TRT_MOREDG_ANTICORPS_MONOCLONAL.name: Monoclonal Antibodies
TRT_MOREDG_ANTICORPS_MONOCLONAL.description: Monoclonal antibodies (mAb or moAb) are antibodies that are made by identical immune cells that are all clones of a unique parent cell. Monoclonal antibodies can have monovalent affinity, in that they bind to the same epitope (the part of an antigen that is recognized by the antibody).

TRT_MOREDG_DEFEROXAMINE.name: Trivalent Cation Chelator
TRT_MOREDG_DEFEROXAMINE.description: A trivalent cation chelator is capable of fixing free iron from plasma or cells to form the ferrioxamine complex.

TRT_MOREDG_IGP.name: GBI
TRT_MOREDG_IGP.description: Glycolipid Biosynthesis Inhibitors prevent glycolipid synthesis.

TRT_MOREDG_TTT_ENZ_SUBSTITU.name: ERT
TRT_MOREDG_TTT_ENZ_SUBSTITU.description: Enzyme replacement therapy (ERT) is a medical treatment which replaces an enzyme that is deficient or absent in the body.

TRT_MOREDG_PHLÉBOTOMIES.name: Phlebotomy
TRT_MOREDG_PHLÉBOTOMIES.description: The surgical opening or puncture of a vein in order to withdraw blood, to introduce a fluid, or (historically) when letting blood.

TRT_MOREDG_DANAPAROÏDE_SODIQUE.name: Danaparoid Sodium
TRT_MOREDG_DANAPAROÏDE_SODIQUE.description: Danaparoid sodium is an anticoagulant with an antithrombotic action due to inhibition of thrombin generation (TGI) by two mechanisms: indirect inactivation of Factor Xa via AT and direct inhibition of thrombin activation of Factor IX (an important feedback loop for thrombin generation).

TRT_MOREDG_REMPLACEMENT_VALVULAIRE.name: Valve Replacement
TRT_MOREDG_REMPLACEMENT_VALVULAIRE.description: Valve replacement surgery is the replacement of one or more of the heart valves with either an artificial heart valve or a bioprosthesis.

TRT_MOREDG_PACEMAKER.name: Pace-Maker
TRT_MOREDG_PACEMAKER.description: A cardiac pacemaker (or artificial pacemaker, so as not to be confused with the natural pacemaker of the heart), is a medical device that generates electrical impulses delivered by electrodes to cause the heart muscle chambers (the upper, or atria and/or the lower, or ventricles) to contract and therefore pump blood; by doing so this device replaces and/or regulates the function of the electrical conduction system of the heart.

TRT_MOREDG_DAI.name: Implantable Cardioverter-Defibrillator
TRT_MOREDG_DAI.description: An implantable cardioverter-defibrillator (ICD) or automated implantable cardioverter defibrillator (AICD) is a device implantable inside the body, able to perform cardioversion, defibrillation, and (in modern versions) pacing of the heart. The device is therefore capable of correcting most life-threatening cardiac arrhythmias. The ICD is the first-line treatment and prophylactic therapy for patients at risk for sudden cardiac death due to ventricular fibrillation and ventricular tachycardia. Current devices can be programmed to detect abnormal heart rhythms and deliver therapy via programmable antitachycardia pacing in addition to low-energy and high-energy shocks.

TRT_MOREDG_REVASCULARISATION.name: Revascularization
TRT_MOREDG_REVASCULARISATION.description: Vascular bypass and angioplasty are the two primary means of revascularization.

TRT_MOREDG_APONEVROTOMIE_DECHARGE.name: Fasciotomy
TRT_MOREDG_APONEVROTOMIE_DECHARGE.description: Fasciotomy or fasciectomy is a surgical procedure where the fascia is cut to relieve tension or pressure commonly to treat the resulting loss of circulation to an area of tissue or muscle.

TRT_MOREDG_MISE_A_PLAT.name: Stent
TRT_MOREDG_MISE_A_PLAT.description: Exclusion of the aneurysmal area from the circulatory flow by interposition of a vascular prosthesis. In the graft flattening, the prosthetic tube is sutured both above and below the aneurysm in order to reconstruct the artery.

TRT_MOREDG_SURRENALECTOMIE.name: Adrenalectomy
TRT_MOREDG_SURRENALECTOMIE.description: Surgical removal of one or both adrenal glands.

TRT_MOREDG_EXERESE_GS.name: Exerese
TRT_MOREDG_EXERESE_GS.description: An excision is a surgical procedure consisting in removing from the organism an element which is harmful or useless to it.

TRT_MOREDG_THYROIDECTOMIE.name: Thyroidectomy
TRT_MOREDG_THYROIDECTOMIE.description: Surgical removal of the thyroid gland.

TRT_MOREDG_EXERESE_MI.name: Exerese
TRT_MOREDG_EXERESE_MI.description: An excision is a surgical procedure consisting in removing from the organism an element which is harmful or useless to it.

TRT_MOREDG_STÉRÉOTAXIE.name: Stereotactic
TRT_MOREDG_STÉRÉOTAXIE.description: Stereotactic neurosurgery is the technique for locating targets of surgical interest within the brain relative to an external frame of reference.
